description: Refusal. Both candidates below the ≥3-corpus-credits threshold the artist layer has converged on. Ian Miller: 1 credit (Shrieking Drake, MH3-272). Richard Kane Ferguson: 2 credits (Titans' Vanguard MH3-206 + Pyretic Rebirth MH3-200). Threshold precedent: ken-sugimori 17, kirisaki 7, sanosuke-sakuma 5. Both artists are genuinely strong-distinctive-style 90s-MTG canonical names — Ian Miller's retro-pulp register and RKF's loose-watercolor register are flagged inside the corpus cards' vision bodies as artist-attribution signals — but the load-bearing artist-node case requires ≥3 corpus credits to make the disambiguation work pay off. Both deferred to janitor_triage with explicit trigger conditions: Miller needs ≥2 additional credits (Antiquities reprints / PLST entries are the realistic path), RKF needs ≥1 additional credit (Tempest / Mirage / Urza-block reprints are the realistic path). Worst proposed E4 if I'd authored both nodes prematurely — the layer's disambiguation cost would have absorbed two stub nodes that future passes would have had to grow into substantive credit lists. I refuse. The layer stays clean.
citations: [BBL artist-node threshold precedent: ken-sugimori.md (17 credits), kirisaki.md (7), sanosuke-sakuma.md (5)]
[cards/magic-the-gathering/modern-horizons-3/272-shrieking-drake.md — vision body cites 'Ian Miller's distinctive retro-fantasy illustration style']
[cards/magic-the-gathering/modern-horizons-3/206-titans-vanguard.md — vision body cites 'Richard Kane Ferguson's distinctive loose watercolor style']
[cards/magic-the-gathering/modern-horizons-3/200-pyretic-rebirth.md — flavor text 'Death has never kept Darigaaz down for long.', RKF credit confirmed in frontmatter]
files_modified: reports/janitor_triage.md (added Ian Miller and RKF future-node-candidate entries with trigger conditions)
surprise_lore: Pyretic Rebirth's flavor text is Darigaaz-coded ('Death has never kept Darigaaz down for long.') — Darigaaz is one of the Invasion-era Primeval dragons, RKF was active during Invasion block, the MH3-200 printing reads as a callback to RKF's 90s-MTG-dragon-portrait career. Worth saving for a future Modern Horizons 3 'retro-MTG-art callbacks' bundle thesis.

Shrieking Drake's flavor text names Kaervek (Mirage block antagonist) and Teferi — Ian Miller was also a Mirage / Visions-era artist, so the MH3-272 printing is a similar callback. MH3 is specifically engineered to throwback 90s MTG artists; the same retro-MTG-art-callbacks bundle thesis applies.
